events.title: WHO alerted to a high-mortality illness cluster in Mongbwalu Health Zone, Ituri Province
DRC and Uganda both declare Ebola outbreaks after INRB confirms Bundibugyo virus in DRC samples; Uganda reports an imported case in Kampala
WHO confirms 8 lab-confirmed DRC cases plus 2 in Kampala; outbreak meets PHEIC criteria
WHO declares a Public Health Emergency of International Concern (PHEIC); CDC opens its emergency response
US begins restricting entry for recent travelers from DRC, Uganda, and South Sudan
American missionary doctor evacuated from DRC to Charité hospital in Berlin for treatment
US tightens entry restrictions further to US citizens/nationals only from affected countries
WHO recommends against using the Zaire-targeted Ervebo vaccine for this outbreak, citing insufficient cross-protection evidence
Confirmed cases reach 378 (363 DRC, 15 Uganda); 63 confirmed deaths
Uganda reports its last new case to date; WHO and Africa CDC launch a joint continental preparedness plan
Outbreak surpasses 850 confirmed cases and ~200 deaths, spreading into North Kivu and South Kivu provinces
WHO issues its first comprehensive clinical care guidelines covering all Ebola/Marburg filovirus strains
DRC confirms over 1,000 cases, making this the third-largest Ebola outbreak on record
First Ebola case confirmed in the EU/EEA: a humanitarian doctor diagnosed in France after returning from DRC
ECDC reports 1,155 DRC cases and 304 deaths, plus 20 Uganda cases and 2 deaths

search_correlation.interpretation_note: Google Trends scores are normalized to the peak within the queried window, not absolute volume. A negative rho here often reflects an early attention spike followed by decay, not declining concern - compare against the search interest chart before drawing conclusions.
